definition: Really, really big. Much bigger than normal.
example: The elephant was enormous compared to the little mouse.

definition: Not afraid to do something scary or hard.
example: She was very brave when she climbed the tall tree.

definition: To find something for the first time.
example: We went to the beach and discovered a beautiful shell.

definition: Any living animal or being.
example: We saw a tiny creature hiding under the rock.

definition: To talk very, very quietly.
example: She had to whisper in the library so she wouldn't disturb anyone.

definition: Wanting to know or learn about something.
example: The curious cat peeked inside the box.

definition: An exciting trip or experience where something new happens.
example: Going to the forest was a great adventure for our family.

definition: To keep someone or something safe from danger.
example: The mother bird protects her babies in the nest.

definition: Tasting really, really good.
example: Grandma made a delicious cake for my birthday.

definition: To make a picture in your mind of something that isn't there.
example: Close your eyes and imagine you are flying like a bird.

definition: Something very special and valuable.
example: The pirate found a treasure chest full of gold coins.

definition: Being soft and careful, not rough.
example: Be gentle when you hold the baby bunny.

definition: To disappear or go away suddenly.
example: The magician made the rabbit vanish from the hat.

definition: Very bright or very smart.
example: The stars looked brilliant in the dark night sky.

definition: A long trip from one place to another.
example: The journey to Grandma's house took three hours.

definition: To go to new places and look around to learn about them.
example: Let's explore the garden and see what bugs we can find.

definition: Very, very old, from a long time ago.
example: The dinosaurs lived in ancient times.

definition: To do something special and fun for a happy reason.
example: We celebrate my birthday with a party every year.

definition: A mountain that can shoot out hot melted rock and smoke.
example: The volcano erupted and lava flowed down the side.

definition: Something you cannot see even though it is there.
example: The wind is invisible but you can feel it on your face.

definition: To gather things together and keep them.
example: I like to collect colorful rocks from the park.

definition: A huge group of stars, planets, and space dust all together.
example: Our planet Earth is in a galaxy called the Milky Way.

definition: So wonderful and beautiful that it makes you say 'wow.'
example: The rainbow after the rain was absolutely magnificent.

definition: To build or put something together.
example: We used blocks to construct a tall tower.

definition: A piece of land with water all around it.
example: We took a boat to visit the island in the lake.

definition: Not joking or being silly; meaning what you say.
example: Dad had a serious look on his face when he talked about safety.

definition: To move from one place to another, usually far away.
example: Birds migrate south when the weather gets cold.

definition: A very, very large body of salt water.
example: Whales swim in the deep blue ocean.

definition: Very strong and a little scary.
example: The fierce lion roared loudly in the jungle.

definition: To make something new that wasn't there before.
example: I love to create pictures with my crayons.

definition: All the bones inside your body that hold you up.
example: We learned about the human skeleton in science class.

definition: Easy to break, so you need to be careful with it.
example: Be careful with that glass vase because it is very fragile.

definition: To shake a little because you are cold or scared.
example: I started to shiver when I forgot my jacket outside.

definition: A big round object in space that moves around a star.
example: Earth is the planet where we all live.

definition: Having lots of bright and pretty colors.
example: The parrot had colorful feathers of red, blue, and green.

definition: To keep yourself steady without falling over.
example: She tried to balance on one foot without wobbling.

definition: A thick forest in a warm place with lots of trees and animals.
example: Monkeys swing from tree to tree in the jungle.

definition: So smooth or wet that you might slide or fall.
example: The floor was slippery after Mom mopped it.

definition: To find out how big, long, or heavy something is.
example: We used a ruler to measure how tall the plant grew.

definition: The remains of a plant or animal from millions of years ago, found in rock.
example: We found a fossil of a leaf pressed into the stone.

definition: Being able to wait calmly without getting upset.
example: You need to be patient while waiting for your turn on the swing.

definition: To throw or spread things in many directions.
example: The wind made the leaves scatter all over the yard.

definition: A design that repeats over and over.
example: The zebra has a pattern of black and white stripes.

definition: Warm, soft, and comfortable.
example: I feel cozy when I wrap up in my favorite blanket.

definition: To save someone or something from danger.
example: The firefighter came to rescue the kitten stuck in the tree.

definition: A dark shape made when something blocks the light.
example: My shadow got really long in the afternoon sun.

definition: Feeling happy about something good you did.
example: Mom was so proud of me when I learned to tie my shoes.

definition: To join or stick one thing to another.
example: I used glue to attach the picture to my poster.

definition: A tool that shows you which direction is north, south, east, or west.
example: The hiker used a compass to find her way through the woods.

definition: To shake a little bit, usually because you are scared or cold.
example: The little mouse started to tremble when it saw the cat.

definition: To move from side to side in an unsteady way.
example: The baby penguin started to wobble as it tried to walk.

definition: The natural home where an animal or plant lives.
example: A pond is the habitat for frogs and fish.

definition: Moving in a smooth and beautiful way.
example: The ballet dancer was very graceful on stage.

definition: To act like something is real when it is not.
example: Let's pretend we are astronauts flying to the moon.

definition: A tool that makes faraway things look closer.
example: We used a telescope to look at the moon and the stars.

definition: Strange and hard to explain or understand.
example: We heard a mysterious sound coming from the attic.

definition: To run very fast, like a horse.
example: The horse began to gallop across the field.

definition: A big storm with loud thunder, lightning, and lots of rain.
example: We stayed inside during the loud thunderstorm.

definition: Extremely big, even bigger than big.
example: The gigantic whale was the biggest animal I ever saw.

definition: To work together with others to get something done.
example: We had to cooperate to build the puzzle as a team.

definition: A small, fuzzy creature that turns into a butterfly.
example: The caterpillar munched on green leaves all day long.

definition: Very, very scared.
example: The puppy was terrified of the loud fireworks.

definition: To change from one thing into something completely different.
example: A caterpillar can transform into a beautiful butterfly.

definition: A clear, shiny stone or piece of ice with flat sides.
example: The crystal sparkled when the sunlight hit it.

definition: So tired that you can barely move.
example: After running in the race, I was completely exhausted.

definition: To watch something carefully to learn about it.
example: We sat quietly to observe the birds building their nest.

definition: When the ground shakes because rocks deep underground move.
example: The earthquake made the dishes rattle on the shelf.

definition: Not wanting to change your mind or do what others ask.
example: The stubborn mule refused to walk any further.

definition: To share information by talking, writing, or showing.
example: Dolphins communicate with each other using clicking sounds.

definition: A group of stars that makes a picture or shape in the sky.
example: We found the Big Dipper constellation in the night sky.

definition: Feeling sleepy and ready for a nap.
example: The warm sunshine made the kitten feel drowsy.

definition: When a flower opens up its petals.
example: The roses bloom every spring in our garden.

definition: An animal that hunts and eats other animals.
example: The hawk is a predator that hunts mice and small birds.

definition: Strange or unusual in an interesting way.
example: The platypus is a very peculiar animal with a duck-like bill.

definition: To fall and roll around.
example: The puppies tumble over each other when they play.

definition: A tool that makes tiny things look bigger so you can see them.
example: We used a microscope to look at a drop of pond water.

definition: So bright and sparkly that it's hard to look at.
example: The dazzling fireworks lit up the whole sky.

definition: To sleep through the whole winter like some animals do.
example: Bears hibernate in a cozy den during the cold months.

definition: A place where water flows down from a high place to a low place.
example: We hiked through the forest and found a beautiful waterfall.

definition: Shining with small flashes of light.
example: The sparkling snow looked like it was covered in glitter.

definition: To jump quickly on top of something to catch it.
example: The kitten likes to pounce on the toy mouse.

definition: A round house made out of blocks of hard snow.
example: People in the Arctic used to build an igloo to stay warm.

definition: Feeling cranky and in a bad mood.
example: My little brother gets grumpy when he misses his nap.

definition: To press something tightly together.
example: I love to squeeze my teddy bear in a big hug.

definition: A person who draws plans for buildings.
example: The architect designed a house with a big backyard.

definition: A feeling on your skin that makes you want to scratch.
example: The mosquito bite on my arm was really itchy.

definition: To send something up into the air or into space.
example: We watched them launch the rocket into the sky.

definition: A big pile of snow sliding quickly down a mountain.
example: The loud noise caused an avalanche on the mountain.

definition: Soft and easy to squish or squeeze.
example: The squishy marshmallow felt so soft in my hand.

definition: To change from solid to liquid, usually because of heat.
example: The ice cream started to melt on the hot summer day.

definition: One of the things you mix together to make food.
example: Flour is an important ingredient for making cookies.

definition: Very wet and soft, usually in a yucky way.
example: My cereal got soggy because I waited too long to eat it.

definition: When a little plant begins to grow from a seed.
example: After we watered the seeds, a tiny green sprout appeared.

definition: One of the seven big pieces of land on Earth.
example: Africa is a continent with many different countries.

definition: Soft, light, and puffy like a cloud.
example: The fluffy bunny had the softest fur I ever touched.

definition: To trip or almost fall while walking.
example: I stumbled on a rock but caught myself before falling.

definition: A special room where scientists do experiments.
example: The scientist mixed colorful liquids in the laboratory.

definition: So amazing and impressive that everyone stops to look.
example: The sunset over the ocean was absolutely spectacular.

definition: To eat something very quickly because you are so hungry.
example: The hungry dog devoured his dinner in seconds.

definition: Something you hold over your head to keep dry in the rain.
example: Don't forget your umbrella because it might rain today.
